hypothesis: Keeping `lowrank_two_stage_residual` fixed at `K=16` and reducing only `EXPANSION_FACTOR` from 12 to 8 will test whether this family can add a lower-cost non-dominated point without giving up too much FVU.
summary: Use a parameter-only probe on the existing `lowrank_two_stage_residual` family at `K=16`, changing only `EXPANSION_FACTOR` to `8` while pinning `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `LR=1.6e-3`, and `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0`. The low-K tail has already been mapped from `K=32` down to `K=1`, so the next clean frontier question is whether the same family can trade some reconstruction quality for a meaningfully smaller model at a proven useful K.
self_review: This avoids the prior policy-rejected code-plus-architecture move, preserves strict variable isolation with `primary_variable=expansion_factor`, and probes the remaining frontier dimension on a stable family.
notes_to_memory: Round 9 shifts from the completed K sweep to an EF sweep on the same family, using `K=16` as the anchor and changing only `EXPANSION_FACTOR`.

The previous routed-family proposal was policy-rejected because it coupled code editing with an architecture change, so the next step should stay parameter-only and isolate a single frontier dimension.
next_hypotheses: If `K=16, EF=8` stays competitive, continue to `K=24, EF=8` or `K=8, EF=8` to map the lower-EF lane.
If `EF=8` degrades too much at `K=16`, keep `EF=12` for this family and revisit architecture changes only after a clean code-only integration plan.
If `EF=8` is borderline but promising, test `EF=10` at the same `K=16` to refine the cost-quality knee.
